enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences with the correct reflexive pronoun: myself, yourself, himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, themselves.

questions.texte: I taught ______ how to use this new smartphone.
She hurt ______ while fixing the computer.
We enjoyed ______ at the technology exhibition.
The robot switched ______ off automatically.
They repaired the computer ______ without any help.
Please, help ______ to some refreshments while you wait.
He made the video ______ using his phone.

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct form of 'going to' and the verb in brackets.

questions.texte: I ______ (buy) a new laptop next week.
She ______ (not / use) her old phone anymore.
They ______ (visit) a technology fair tomorrow.
______ you ______ (download) that new app?
We ______ (not / watch) TV tonight; we'll play video games.
He ______ (learn) how to code next month.
What ______ you ______ (do) after school?

enonce.texte:
Read the following dialogue between two students about technology and answer the questions below.

Dialogue:

Marie: Hi John! Are you going to buy a new phone soon?

John: Yes, I am. I'm going to buy the new model next week.

Marie: That's great! What features are you looking for?

John: I want a phone with a good camera and long battery life.

Marie: Have you considered the brand that my brother has? He bought it himself.

John: Oh, I'll check it out. By the way, are you going to come to the technology exhibition tomorrow?

Marie: Yes, I am. I'm going to meet some friends there.

John: Great! I'll see you there!

questions.texte: What is John going to buy next week?
questions.reponse: He is going to buy a new phone (the new model).

questions.texte: What features does John want in his new phone?
questions.reponse: He wants a good camera and long battery life.

questions.texte: Identify the reflexive pronoun used in the dialogue.
questions.reponse: The reflexive pronoun used is 'himself' (in the sentence: 'He bought it himself').

questions.texte: What is Marie going to do tomorrow?
questions.reponse: She is going to go to the technology exhibition and meet some friends there.

enonce.texte:
Create a dialogue (8-10 exchanges) between two friends discussing a new gadget they want to buy. In your dialogue, you must include:

  • At least two reflexive pronouns (myself, yourself, himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, themselves)
  • At least two examples of 'going to' to express the future
  • Information about the gadget's features

questions.texte: Write the dialogue between the two friends.
questions.reponse:
Paul: Hey Sarah! Are you going to buy a tablet soon?

Sarah: Yes, I am. I'm going to save my money and buy one next month.

Paul: That sounds great! Which model are you going to choose?

Sarah: I want one with a big screen and fast processor. I'm going to use it for my studies and drawing.

Paul: Have you seen the new model that came out? It has a very high resolution.

Sarah: Yes, I looked at it myself online yesterday. It looks amazing!

Paul: I bought myself a tablet last year and I use it every day.

Sarah: Are you going to help me choose the right one?

Paul: Of course! I'm going to go with you to the store.

enonce.texte:
Write a short presentation (about 80-100 words) about a gadget you are going to buy in the future. In your presentation, include:

  • The name of the gadget
  • Why you are going to buy it
  • Its main features
  • At least two reflexive pronouns
  • At least two sentences with 'going to'

questions.texte: Write the presentation about the gadget you are going to buy.
questions.reponse:
My Future Laptop

I am going to buy a new laptop next month. I am going to choose a lightweight model with a long battery life. The laptop will have a powerful processor and a high-resolution screen. I am going to use it for my school work, watching movies, and playing games. I will teach myself how to use some advanced programs. I hope I can buy it myself with my savings. The laptop I am going to buy is also very portable, so I can carry it to school easily. I am very excited about this purchase. I think it will help me improve my skills and enjoy my leisure time.

intitule: Error Correction: Reflexive Pronouns and Future Tense

enonce.texte: Identify and correct the errors in the following sentences. Each sentence has one error related to reflexive pronouns or the use of 'going to'.

questions.texte: I am going to fixed my computer myself.
questions.reponse: Corrected: I am going to fix my computer myself. ('fixed' should be 'fix' - base form after 'going to')

questions.texte: She hurt herself while she was going to fix the printer.
questions.reponse: Corrected: She hurt herself while she was fixing the printer. (The sentence is correct. No error found.)

questions.texte: They are going to buy themself new phones.
questions.reponse: Corrected: They are going to buy themselves new phones. ('themself' should be 'themselves' - plural reflexive pronoun)

questions.texte: We are going to enjoying ourselves at the tech fair.
questions.reponse: Corrected: We are going to enjoy ourselves at the tech fair. ('enjoying' should be 'enjoy' - base form after 'going to')
